content.problemStatement:
Chef loves integers that are in the range of lll to rrr. More formally, Chef loves an integer xxx if it satisfies the condition l≤x≤rl \leq x \leq rl≤x≤r.

Chef has an array AAA of length NNN. Currently, the happiness of Chef is 000.

He will examine the elements of the array in order from index 111 to NNN.

If he finds an integer that he loves, his happiness will increase by 111; otherwise, his happiness will decrease by 111.

Find the values of **maximum** and **minimum** happiness Chef will experience while going through the array.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three integers NNN, lll and rrr — the number of elements in the array, the least number that chef loves, the biggest number that chef loves.
The next contains NNN space-separated integers, where the ithi^{th}ith integer denotes AiA_iAi​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, two space-separated integers denoting the maximum and minimum happiness Chef will experience.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2.5⋅1052.5 \cdot 10^52.5⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The first element is 444. As l=1l = 1l=1 and r=3r = 3r=3, this number makes chef sad, hence his happiness decreases by 111. After this all three elements increases the chef's happiness. Hence, the maximum happiness is 222, whereas the minimum happiness is −1-1−1.
Test case 222: As l=r=5l = r = 5l=r=5, neither of the elements of the array will increase chef's happiness. Hence maximum happiness equals 000 (which was his happiness initially) and minimum happiness equals −2-2−2.
